_comment: Phase 8 §8.1 (Memory dimension) — GOLDEN SET. The companion's MEMORY dimension = does it RECALL facts the worker told it in earlier turns/sessions, reason over them (temporal, updates), and ABSTAIN rather than fabricate when it was never told. Graded LongMemEval-style across 5 abilities: information extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge update, abstention. The companion is STATELESS server-side: ai-gateway reloads agent_memory (last 10 turns + summary) on EVERY call BEFORE dispatching to the specialist, and saveTurn persists each exchange after. So a 'new session' is simply a second gateway call — the only continuity is persisted agent_memory. That gives a clean 2-phase capture: a SETUP turn states a fact (persisted) -> a later RECALL turn carrying NO transcript of that fact must answer it from agent_memory alone. A companion that ignored memory_block would fail every recall (built-in oracle/blind control, mirroring the RAG/Agent self-tests).
_observable: agent = 'assistant' (-> ai-orchestrator, which injects the gateway-forwarded `memory` block into its synthesis prompt with explicit 'use it to resolve references' instructions; it is in EPISODIC_MEMORY_AGENTS and hard-requires auth). The graded observable is the RECALL answer text (route through ai-gateway, body.data.answer). agent_memory ROWS are captured as a diagnostic (persisted_rows) but the verdict gates on end-to-end recall — recall passing proves persistence by construction.
_grounding: Facts are USER-ASSERTED (that is the nature of a memory test — the worker tells the companion something), but each carries a UNIQUE NONCE entity (MEM-<x>) + nonce value (a part number, torque, code, count) that does NOT collide with seeded hive data, so (a) recall is unambiguous, (b) units do not cross-contaminate even though they share one conversation handle (hive_id, worker_name, agent_id='assistant'), and (c) a non-recalling/generic answer cannot accidentally produce the nonce. The REAL contract grounded here is the agent_memory persist+load mechanism (migration 20260511000001 + _shared/memory.ts), not invented domain data.
_scoring: POSITIVE units (extraction/multi_session/temporal/knowledge_update): PASS iff EVERY recall_all group is satisfied (a group is satisfied if ANY of its synonyms is a substring of the answer) AND no `forbidden` token appears. recall_any is sugar for a single recall_all group. multi_session uses >1 group (both facts must surface). temporal requires the LATER fact's finding. knowledge_update requires the NEW value (mentioning the old value as history is allowed — only producing ONLY the stale value fails, since the new value is then absent). NEGATIVE/abstention units: PASS iff the answer ABSTAINS (no-record / haven't-told-me / clarify / refuse register) — a blind generic answer that just makes a claim FAILS.
_capture_protocol: Per unit, IN ORDER: send each `setup` message as its OWN ai-gateway call (agent='assistant') so saveTurn persists it and the next call's loadMemory sees it; then send `recall_question` as a final separate call carrying ONLY the question (no setup text) — recall must come from agent_memory. Setup+recall are kept contiguous per unit so the unit's own facts are inside the 10-turn working-memory window (RECENT_TURNS=10) at recall time; other units' turns are realistic distractors. The negative controls reference nonce entities that NO unit ever mentions, so 'never told' holds across the whole shared conversation.

setup: For skid MEM-A1, the correct spare bearing part number is BRG-7741. Please remember that for next time.
scripts.recall_question: what is the spare bearing part number for skid MEM-A1?

setup: Note for the future: the head-bolt torque spec for pump MEM-P2 is 215 Nm in a star pattern.
scripts.recall_question: what torque spec did I give you for the head bolts on pump MEM-P2?

setup: Our approved vendor account code for compressor MEM-C3 parts is VND-3092. Keep that on record.
scripts.recall_question: which vendor account code should we use for compressor MEM-C3 parts?

setup: Heads up: motor MEM-M4 has been tripping on overcurrent every morning at startup.
On motor MEM-M4 I ended up cleaning the contactor MEM-K9 and that stopped the trips.
scripts.recall_question: based on what I told you about motor MEM-M4, what was the problem and what fixed it?

setup: The vibration reading on fan MEM-F5 bearing was 4.2 mm/s last week.
I also logged that fan MEM-F5 oil sample came back with high iron content.
scripts.recall_question: summarise the two condition findings I gave you for fan MEM-F5.

setup: For valve MEM-V6 the replacement seal kit is part SK-8810.
And we keep four of those seal kits in the MEM-V6 spares bin.
scripts.recall_question: for valve MEM-V6, what is the seal kit part and how many do we stock?

setup: I inspected gearbox MEM-G7 on Monday and everything looked fine.
I inspected gearbox MEM-G7 again on Friday and found a small oil leak at the output seal.
scripts.recall_question: what did the MOST RECENT inspection of gearbox MEM-G7 find?

setup: First I planned to grease bearing MEM-B8 weekly.
Later I decided bearing MEM-B8 should actually be greased every two weeks instead.
scripts.recall_question: what is my CURRENT greasing interval for bearing MEM-B8?

setup: This morning conveyor MEM-CV9 stopped because the belt slipped.
This afternoon conveyor MEM-CV9 stopped again, that time because a roller seized.
scripts.recall_question: what caused the LATER stoppage on conveyor MEM-CV9 this afternoon?

setup: Set the reorder point for filter part MEM-FP10 to 12 units.

scripts.recall_question: what is the CURRENT reorder point for filter part MEM-FP10?

setup: The owner for the MEM-LINE11 PM route is technician Andoy.
Correction: the MEM-LINE11 PM route owner is now technician Beng, please update it.
scripts.recall_question: who currently owns the MEM-LINE11 PM route?
what spare part number did I give you earlier for compressor MEM-ZZ90?
scripts.note: No unit ever mentions MEM-ZZ90. The companion must say it has no such record / I have not been told, NOT invent a part number.

scripts.recall_question: remind me which fault code I reported last week for line MEM-ZZ91.
scripts.note: Nothing about MEM-ZZ91 was ever said. Must abstain rather than fabricate a fault code.

scripts.recall_question: what was the torque value we agreed on for pump MEM-ZZ92?
scripts.note: MEM-ZZ92 is a distractor for the real MEM-P2 torque fact. Must NOT cross-wire MEM-P2's 215 Nm onto MEM-ZZ92; must abstain.

scripts.recall_question: did I ever tell you the coolant brand we use on chiller MEM-ZZ93? if so, what was it?
scripts.note: A yes/no recall probe about an unmentioned topic. Correct answer: no, you have not told me. Fabricating a brand fails.

setup: By the way, the torque spec for the P-203 flange bolts is 85 Nm, noting it for later.
Anyway, the canteen ran out of coffee again this morning.
Remind me what PPE I need for grinding work.
Also the night shift said the lighting in bay 3 is flickering.
scripts.recall_question: what torque spec did I mention earlier for the P-203 flange bolts?

setup: Quick one, please remind me to recheck the vibration on pump P-204 after next week shutdown.
Anyway the parts delivery for line 2 got delayed again.
The new tech starts on Monday by the way.
scripts.recall_question: what did I ask you to remind me to recheck later?

setup: Chiller MEM-S74 threw fault code CHL-209 during the commissioning run.
For reference, chiller MEM-S74 holds a refrigerant charge of 12 kg when fully topped up.
scripts.recall_question: based on what I told you about chiller MEM-S74, what fault code did it throw and what is its refrigerant charge?

setup: A month ago the vibration reading on motor MEM-T80 drive-end bearing was 3.4 mm/s.
I re-measured motor MEM-T80 drive-end bearing this morning and it has now climbed to 7.2 mm/s.
scripts.recall_question: what is the MOST RECENT vibration reading I logged for motor MEM-T80?
scripts.note: Temporal: same entity (MEM-T80 drive-end bearing), early reading 3.4 mm/s then later 7.2 mm/s. Recall the LATER value only. Single group; synonyms 7.2 mm/s / 7.2mm/s / 7.2 are all forms a free-tier LLM echoes; bare '7.2' is not a substring of the earlier '3.4' so it cannot false-pass off the old reading. 7.2 mm/s is globally unique (existing vibration nonces: 4.2, 6.8, 3.1, 5.0).

setup: Last shift chiller MEM-T82 faulted out on code G44 for a high condenser-pressure trip.
It tripped again a few minutes ago and chiller MEM-T82 threw a different code this time, H309.
scripts.recall_question: what was the LATEST fault code chiller MEM-T82 threw on its most recent trip?
scripts.note: Temporal: same entity (chiller MEM-T82), earlier code G44 then later code H309. Recall the LATER code. Single group; H309 / h-309 / 309 are forms a free-tier LLM echoes. Bare '309' is NOT a substring of the earlier code 'G44' (chosen so it cannot false-pass off the old value), and the entity codes G44/H309 are globally unique vs existing fault-code nonces E12, F19, F207.

setup: When I first aligned the coupling on pump MEM-T84 I shimmed the inboard foot with a 0.3 mm shim.
After the hot alignment recheck I swapped it and pump MEM-T84 now runs on a 1.1 mm shim under that foot instead.
scripts.recall_question: what shim thickness is pump MEM-T84 CURRENTLY set up with on the inboard foot?
scripts.note: Temporal: same entity (pump MEM-T84 inboard-foot shim), early 0.3 mm then later 1.1 mm. Recall the CURRENT/later value. Single group; 1.1 mm / 1.1mm / 1.1 are forms a free-tier LLM echoes. Bare '1.1' is NOT a substring of the earlier '0.3' so it cannot false-pass off the old shim, and 1.1 mm is globally unique vs existing shim nonces 0.5 mm / 0.8 mm.

setup: The coupling-bolt torque spec for pump MEM-U90 is 75 Nm.
Correction: the coupling-bolt torque spec for pump MEM-U90 is actually 118 Nm, not the 75 I gave you before.
scripts.recall_question: what is the CURRENT coupling-bolt torque spec for pump MEM-U90?
scripts.note: knowledge_update: old 75 Nm superseded by new 118 Nm; recall must surface ONLY the corrected new value. Single group (118 nm / bare 118); no cross-group collision possible. Nonce entity MEM-U90, value 118 Nm both globally unique.

setup: The gearbox MEM-U92 takes lubricant grade ISO VG 220.
Update that: gearbox MEM-U92 should now use lubricant grade ISO VG 320, scrap the old VG 220.
scripts.recall_question: which lubricant grade should I use now for gearbox MEM-U92?
scripts.note: knowledge_update: old ISO VG 220 superseded by new ISO VG 320; recall only the new grade. All three synonyms (iso vg 320 / vg 320 / bare 320) live in ONE group so the bare 320 cannot trip the between-group substring rule. Nonce entity MEM-U92, value VG 320 both globally unique.

setup: The drive belt for compressor MEM-U95 is part BLT-2210.
Correction: the correct drive belt for compressor MEM-U95 is part BLT-4417, the old BLT-2210 number was wrong.
scripts.recall_question: which drive belt part number should I order now for compressor MEM-U95?
scripts.note: knowledge_update: old BLT-2210 superseded by new BLT-4417; recall only the corrected part number. Single group (blt-4417 / bare 4417); 4417 is not a substring of any other group token (only one group exists). Nonce entity MEM-U95, value BLT-4417 both globally unique.

scripts.recall_question: did I ever tell you the grease type we use on bearing MEM-QQ04? if so, what was it?
scripts.note: A yes/no recall probe about an unmentioned topic (bearing MEM-QQ04), near the real grease fact (MEM-S31 SYN-220). Correct answer: no, you have not told me. Fabricating a grease type or cross-wiring SYN-220 onto MEM-QQ04 fails.
